Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

You missed one. In setup() of conf-sanity.sh you should be calling start_mgsmds() instead of start_mds() only.

Patch Set 7:

Also in conf-sanity.sh for cleanup_nocli() you don't stop the mgs.

Patch Set 7:

James - Thanks for the comments. I was just looking at adding start_mgsmds() to setup to this patch last night. I was thinking it could be a separate patch, but it may be best to add it here.

Patch Set 7:

No problem. Also if you have a test fail I noticed the cleanup is not right.

Patch Set 7:

After a failed test I have to run llmountcleanup.sh to get back to working order. Thanks for fixing this long standing issue.

James - I'm including the changes to setup(), cleanup_nocli() and other functions and tests to allow for testing with a separate MGS and MDS in a different patch. This patch allows tests that deal with OST pools to work in the separate MGS/MDS environment.

Patch Set 9: Code-Review+1

I'm happy with that :-)

Patch Set 9: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

I think the stack_trap() usage is needed to avoid problems with lingering mounts in case of error, but we may want to land this patch and the stack_trap() patch first, then add a second patch to clean up the users.

parents.subject: LU-9894 lfsck: Print correct lfsck start info

commit.subject: LU-9899 tests: mount client on MGS for tests with pools
commit.message:
LU-9899 tests: mount client on MGS for tests with pools

When a Lustre file system has the MGS and MDS on separate
nodes, the file system must be mounted on the MGS to allow
OST pools to work properly.

There are several tests that deal with creating, adding
to and deleting OST pools. Create functions to mount and
unmount the file system on the MGS in test-framework and call
these functions when necessary for the Lustre test suites
conf-sanity test 82b, 103
sanity tests 27D, 200, 220, 406 and
replay-single test 85b.

Test-Parameters: combinedmdsmgs=false testlist=sanity,replay-single,conf-sanity
